input: Linear Algebra for Machine Learning: Data structures — Scalar (0D, single number), Vector (1D, array), Matrix (2D, rows × columns), Tensor (3D+, multidimensional). Matrix dimensions always Rows × Columns. Operations: Addition (element-wise), Scalar Multiplication (multiply all by constant), Matrix Multiplication (inner dimensions must match: (A×B)·(B×C)=(A×C), row-column dot products), Transpose (flip rows↔columns, essential for neural network calculations, aligns dimensions for multiplication). Why it matters: forward propagation is matrix multiplication — weighted_sum = (inputs · weights_transpose) + bias. NumPy: np.dot(A,B) for multiplication, A.T for transpose, A.shape for dimensions.
